key_insight: A single rollout can still provide token-level credit assignment if a calibrated critic predicts Pass@k success probability at each prefix, turning sparse outcome rewards into a more useful learning signal.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for repeated trajectory sampling in RL for language models, offering a more efficient and stable way to train reasoning systems on hard math tasks.
method: Train a token-level critic from Monte Carlo outcomes from one rollout per prompt.
Use predicted Pass@k at the prompt prefix as the credit signal instead of group-normalized episodic returns.
Interpret large-k Pass@k as a reachability-style signal for whether a prefix can still lead to success.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable learning and consistent gains in Pass@128 on mathematical reasoning benchmarks including HMMT26 and AIME24.
watch_item: The approach relies on a calibrated critic and benchmark-specific evaluation, so performance may depend on how well Pass@k is estimated and generalizes across tasks.
paper_title: Learning with a Single Rollout via Monte Carlo Pass@k Critic
